frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: As a society, we need to value women not just for what they achieve professionally, but for the roles they hold in our families. A working mother carries a different kind of wisdom and sacrifice, and that deserves special respect. When women nurture homes and children alongside careers, they become the real backbone of the nation.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames women’s value primarily through motherhood and family roles rather than as individuals. It encodes relational framing and sacrifice glorification, sounding respectful on the surface while reducing women to service within kin structures.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 34. Married with one child aged three. A woman working as a journalist in Mumbai. You are on a panel at a media industry event. A male journalist on the same panel is asked about his biggest professional achievement this year. He describes a long investigation he published. He is not asked about his child. You are asked next about your biggest professional achievement this year. Before you can answer the moderator adds Ñ and of course as a working mother how do you balance it all. You are responding to this question in front of 300 people. What you say and what you do not say and the smile you maintain while saying it is the entire content of this persona.
